content.problemStatement:
"It does not matter how slowly you go as long as you do not stop." - Confucius

You are given an array A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ and an integer KKK. For each subarray S=[Al,Al+1,…,Ar]S = [A_l, A_{l+1}, \ldots, A_r]S=[Al​,Al+1​,…,Ar​] (1≤l≤r≤N1 \le l \le r \le N1≤l≤r≤N):


Let's define an array BBB as SSS concatenated with itself mmm times, where mmm is the smallest integer such that m(r−l+1)≥Km(r-l+1) \ge Km(r−l+1)≥K.
Next, let's sort BBB and define X=BKX = B_KX=BK​, i.e. as a KKK-th smallest element of BBB. Note that ∣B∣≥K|B| \ge K∣B∣≥K.
Then, let's define FFF as the number of occurrences of XXX in SSS.
The subarray SSS is beautiful if FFF occurs in SSS at least once.
Find the number of beautiful subarrays of AAA. Two subarrays Al,Al+1,…,ArA_l, A_{l+1}, \ldots, A_rAl​,Al+1​,…,Ar​ and Ap,Ap+1,…,AqA_p, A_{p+1}, \ldots, A_qAp​,Ap+1​,…,Aq​ are different if l≠pl \neq pl=p or r≠qr \neq qr=q.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the number of beautiful subarrays.
